metadata.name: Control SELinux File Contexts
metadata.description: Configure the Apache HTTP server to publish web content from a nonstandard document root
objectives: Configure the Apache HTTP server to publish web content from a nonstandard document root

hosts.name: servera
hosts.description: Web server with custom document root

tasks.name: Verify /custom directory exists
tasks.description: The /custom directory should exist as the nonstandard document root

tasks.name: Verify /custom/index.html exists with correct content
tasks.description: The index.html file should exist with 'This is SERVERA.' content
tasks.failure_message: index.html is missing or has incorrect content
hints: Create the file: echo 'This is SERVERA.' | sudo tee /custom/index.html

tasks.name: Verify Apache is configured to use /custom directory
tasks.description: The httpd.conf should reference /custom as the document root
tasks.success_message: Apache is configured to use /custom directory
tasks.failure_message: Apache is not configured to use /custom directory

tasks.name: Verify httpd service is running
tasks.description: The Apache web service should be running
hints: Start httpd: sudo systemctl start httpd

tasks.name: Verify httpd service is enabled
tasks.description: The Apache web service should be enabled at boot
hints: Enable httpd: sudo systemctl enable httpd
Or use: sudo systemctl enable --now httpd

tasks.name: Verify SELinux file context rule for /custom
tasks.description: An SELinux file context rule should exist for /custom with httpd_sys_content_t
tasks.success_message: SELinux file context rule is configured for /custom
tasks.failure_message: SELinux file context rule is missing for /custom
hints: Add the rule: sudo semanage fcontext -a -t httpd_sys_content_t '/custom(/.*)?'

tasks.name: Verify /custom has httpd_sys_content_t context
tasks.description: The /custom directory should have the httpd_sys_content_t SELinux context applied

tasks.name: Verify /custom/index.html has httpd_sys_content_t context
tasks.description: The index.html file should have the httpd_sys_content_t SELinux context
tasks.failure_message: index.html does not have httpd_sys_content_t context

tasks.name: Verify web content is accessible via HTTP
tasks.description: The web page at http://servera/index.html should return the expected content
tasks.failure_message: Web content is not accessible - check httpd status and SELinux contexts
hints: Check httpd status: sudo systemctl status httpd
